import.invalid_file: Could not read the uploaded file.
import.missing_header: The CSV file must include a header row.
import.empty_file: The CSV file has no data rows.
import.row_failed: This row could not be imported.

markdown.empty: Nothing to preview yet. Start writing Markdown in the Write tab.

welcome.eyebrow: Product security lifecycle for CRA readiness
welcome.title: CRA Compliance Workspace for small and mid-size software manufacturers
welcome.description: Organizes product portfolios, risks, controls, evidence, and release approvals into one traceable structure for daily work and audit readiness.

modules.organization_description: Roles, permissions, approvals, and accountable ownership.
modules.products_description: Portfolio, versions, support periods, and release history.
modules.risk_description: Links requirements, risks, controls, and evidence.
modules.vulnerabilities_description: Tracks deadlines, corrective actions, and reporting preparation.

welcome.disclaimer: The platform does not promise automatic legal conformity. It supports processes, decisions, and evidence management for CRA readiness.

login.title: Log in to your account
login.description: Enter your email and password below to log in

forgot_password.title: Forgot password
forgot_password.description: Enter your email to receive a password reset link

reset_password.title: Reset password
reset_password.description: Please enter your new password below

verify_email.title: Email verification
verify_email.description: Please verify your email address by clicking on the link we just emailed to you.
verify_email.link_sent: A new verification link has been sent to the email address you provided during registration.

confirm_password.title: Confirm password
confirm_password.description: This is a secure area of the application. Please confirm your password before continuing.

force_password.title: Change your password
force_password.description: Your account requires a password change before continuing.

two_factor_setup.title: Enable two-factor authentication
two_factor_setup.description: Two-factor authentication is required before you can use the workspace.

two_factor_challenge.code_description: Enter the authentication code provided by your authenticator application.
two_factor_challenge.recovery_description: Please confirm access to your account by entering one of your emergency recovery codes.

two_factor.title: Two-factor authentication
two_factor.enable_help: When you enable two-factor authentication, you will be prompted for a secure pin during login. This pin can be retrieved from a TOTP-supported application on your phone.
two_factor.enabled_help: You will be prompted for a secure, random pin during login, which you can retrieve from the TOTP-supported application on your phone.

modal.enabled_description: Two-factor authentication is now enabled. Scan the QR code or enter the setup key in your authenticator app.
modal.verify_description: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app
modal.enable_description: To finish enabling two-factor authentication, scan the QR code or enter the setup key in your authenticator app

recovery.title: 2FA recovery codes
recovery.description: Recovery codes let you regain access if you lose your 2FA device. Store them in a secure password manager.
recovery.hint: Each recovery code can be used once to access your account and will be removed after use. If you need more, click :action above.

users.title: Users
users.subtitle: Manage users and role assignments.
users.force_password: Force password change on first login
users.search_placeholder: Search by name, email, role or ID...
users.confirm_reset_two_factor: This clears the user's authenticator and recovery codes. They must set up 2FA again on their next login.
users.two_factor_not_enabled: This user does not have two-factor authentication enabled.

organizations.title: Organizations
organizations.subtitle: Manage tenant organizations.
organizations.slug_help: Leave empty to generate from the name.
organizations.search_placeholder: Search by name, slug, email or ID...
organizations.seed_starter_controls_help: Recommended. Copies a curated CRA starter set into the organization library. You can edit, deactivate or add more controls later.
organizations.locale_help: Dashboard UI and starter control texts use this language. Default is English.
organizations.locale_help_edit: Changing the language updates starter controls from the catalogue. Custom controls are left unchanged.
organizations.confirm_delete: This permanently deletes the organization, all its products, controls, evidence and related data, and removes member accounts that belong only to this organization. This cannot be undone.

requirements.title: Requirements catalogue
requirements.subtitle: Versioned CRA regulatory requirements
requirements.search_placeholder: Search by code, article or text...

help.code: Stable internal code unique within the regulation.
help.article_ref: Article or annex reference from the CRA text.
help.sort_order: Display order in catalogues and matrices.
help.is_active: Inactive requirements are not added to new product matrices.
help.create_new_version: When enabled, saves content as a new current version instead of editing the current one.
help.requirement_text: Formal requirement wording used for assessments (English).
help.requirement_text_bg: Bulgarian translation of the formal requirement wording.
help.plain_language_bg: Bulgarian short explanation for product teams.
help.applicability_notes_bg: Bulgarian notes on when this requirement typically applies.

users.title: Users
users.subtitle: Manage users in your organization
users.confirm_reset_two_factor: This clears the user's authenticator and recovery codes. They must set up 2FA again on their next login.
users.two_factor_not_enabled: This user does not have two-factor authentication enabled.
users.search_placeholder: Search by name, email, role or ID...

dialog.title: Password for encrypted archive
dialog.description: The export will be packaged in an encrypted 7z archive. Remember the password — it is not stored in the system and is required to open the file.

validation.password_min: The archive password must be at least :min characters.
validation.password_max: The archive password may not be greater than 128 characters.

errors.xlsx_missing: The export file was not generated successfully.
errors.work_dir_failed: Failed to create a temporary directory for the export.
errors.source_missing: The source file for archiving does not exist.
errors.archive_dir_failed: Failed to create a temporary directory for the archive.
errors.archive_failed: Failed to create the encrypted 7z archive: :details
errors.archive_missing: The encrypted archive was not created.
errors.seven_zip_unavailable: 7z is not available on the server. Install p7zip-full and set SEVEN_ZIP_BINARY in .env.

errors.last_owner_role: Cannot change the role of the last organization owner.
errors.last_owner_delete: Cannot delete the last organization owner.

incidents.subtitle: Cross-product incident register for your organization
incidents.search_placeholder: Search by title, product, status or severity...

sdl.subtitle: Cross-product SDL runs and release security gates for your organization
sdl.search_placeholder: Search by title, product, status or stage...

customers.title: Customers
customers.subtitle: Organizations and contacts that run your products
customers.confirm_delete: Delete this customer permanently? Linked installations will also be removed.
customers.search_placeholder: Search by name, contact or reference...

help.name: Display name for this customer organization.
help.primary_contact: Name or email for operational contact.
help.criticality: How critical this customer is for patch and notification priority.
help.is_active: Inactive customers stay in history but are hidden from default pickers later.
help.file: UTF-8 CSV with comma separator. First row must be column headers.

customers.import_subtitle: Bulk create or update customers from a CSV file
customers.columns_hint: Required column: name. Optional: external_ref, primary_contact, criticality (low|medium|high), notes, is_active (1/0). Existing rows match by external_ref or name.
customers.completed: Import finished: :created created, :updated updated, :skipped skipped, :errors row errors.
customers.all_rows_failed: No rows could be imported. Check the CSV format and values.
customers.missing_name_column: The CSV must include a name column.
customers.invalid_criticality: Criticality must be low, medium, or high.

auditor.title: Auditor reviews
auditor.subtitle: Share product compliance packages with auditors and track findings
auditor.review_subtitle: Read-only compliance package: product passport, readiness and selected evidence.
auditor.review_disclaimer: This view is for review only. Product data and readiness are live snapshots — not a frozen export.
auditor.no_package_evidence: No evidence was included in this package.

export.disclaimer: This export is a point-in-time snapshot of the review package: passport summary, readiness, findings and selected evidence files. It is not a legal compliance certificate.
export.evidence_readme: Evidence files included in this package (when a file was uploaded) are stored under the evidence/ folder. Metadata is listed in review-package.pdf.

auditor.shared_with_notifications: Review package shared. Queued :queued auditor email(s); skipped :skipped without email.
auditor.guest_link_help: Create a time-limited magic link so an external auditor can open this package without an account (view-only).
auditor.guest_link_copy_once: Copy this URL now — it is shown only once after generate.
auditor.guest_link_copy_failed: Could not copy the guest link.
auditor.guest_link_only_shared: Guest links can only be generated for shared packages.
auditor.guest_link_missing: No guest link is active for this package.
auditor.confirm_revoke_guest: Revoke the external magic link? Anyone with the URL will lose access immediately.

guest.title: External auditor review
guest.disclaimer: You are viewing a time-limited guest link. This page is read-only and does not require a user account.
guest.view_only_banner: View-only access — findings cannot be added from this link.

auditor.confirm_share: Share this package with auditors? Content becomes read-only for editing.
auditor.confirm_close: Close this shared package? Further findings should not be added.
auditor.search_placeholder: Search by title, product or status...
auditor.only_draft_editable: Only draft packages can be edited.
auditor.only_draft_deletable: Only draft packages can be deleted.
auditor.only_draft_shareable: Only draft packages can be shared.
auditor.only_shared_closable: Only shared packages can be closed.
auditor.product_invalid: Selected product is not valid for this organization.
auditor.no_evidence: No evidence records for this product yet.
auditor.preselected_evidence_hint: :count evidence item(s) were preselected from the product evidence list. Review the selection before creating the package.

help.title: Short name for this review (e.g. CRA readiness review Q3).
help.notes: Describe scope and expectations for the auditor.
help.product: Package is product-scoped; passport and readiness come from this product.
help.evidence: Select evidence items included in this package.

findings.title: Findings
findings.subtitle: Auditor observations and owner remediation status
findings.only_shared_creatable: Findings can only be added to shared packages.
findings.only_shared_editable: Findings can only be edited while the package is shared.
findings.only_shared_deletable: Findings can only be deleted while the package is shared.
findings.only_open_deletable: Only open findings can be deleted.
findings.only_shared_or_closed_status: Remediation status can only be updated on shared or closed packages.

help.title: Short summary of the gap or observation.
help.body: Describe what was found and why it matters for CRA compliance.
help.severity: How serious the finding is for the reviewed product.
help.status: Owner remediation decision for this finding.

policies.title: Policy library
policies.subtitle: Organization policies for disclosure, support, updates and secure development
policies.confirm_delete: Delete this draft or under-review policy?
policies.confirm_retire: Retire this approved policy? A newer version can supersede it later.
policies.search_placeholder: Search by title, type, status or version...
policies.publish_evidence_help: Creates a product evidence record (type: policy) with this policy Markdown body.
policies.publish_no_products: Create a product first, then publish this policy as evidence for it.
policies.cannot_delete_approved: Approved policies cannot be deleted. Retire them instead.
policies.only_draft_submit: Only draft policies can be submitted for review.
policies.only_under_review_approve: Only policies under review can be approved.
policies.only_approved_retire: Only approved policies can be retired.
policies.only_approved_publish: Only approved policies can be published as evidence.
policies.already_published: This policy is already linked to evidence.
policies.publish_product_invalid: Selected product is not valid for this organization.
policies.only_editable_draft_or_review: Only draft or under-review policies can be edited.
policies.supersedes_invalid: Selected previous version is not valid for this policy type.

help.title: Short display name for this policy version.
help.use_template: Prefill title and Markdown body from a starter template for this type.
help.body: Write the policy in Markdown. Approved policies become read-only.
help.product_id: Evidence is product-scoped; choose which product register receives this policy document.
help.submit_product_id: Tasks are product-scoped; choose which product board receives the review task.
help.assignee_user_id: Optional reviewer. Defaults to you if left empty.

policies.related_help: Open the policy library filtered to the policy types most relevant to this item.
policies.preview_empty: Nothing to preview yet. Start writing Markdown in the Write tab.
policies.diff_unchanged: No differences between this version and the previous one.
policies.submit_review_help: Creates an open product task so a reviewer can approve this policy.
policies.submit_no_products: Create a product first, then submit this policy for review.
policies.submit_product_invalid: Selected product is not valid for this organization.
policies.submit_assignee_invalid: Selected assignee is not a member of this organization.
policies.review_task_description:
Organizational policy awaiting approval.
Type: :type
Title: :title
Version: :version

pdf.disclaimer: This export is an organizational policy snapshot for internal or auditor review. It is not a regulatory filing.

controls.title: Controls library
controls.subtitle: How your organization implements CRA requirements
controls.refresh_starter_help: Adds missing starter controls and updates existing starter_template rows from the catalogue. Custom controls are left unchanged.
controls.search_placeholder: Search by code, name or description...

help.code: Stable short code unique within the organization.
help.name: Short name for the control as used by product teams.
help.name_bg: Bulgarian translation of the control name.
help.description: What this control does and why it exists.
help.implementation_guidance: Practical steps for implementing and operating the control.
help.implementation_guidance_bg: Bulgarian translation of the implementation guidance.
help.automation_level: How much of the control is automated versus manual.
help.frequency: How often the control runs or is reviewed.
help.owner: Organization member accountable for this control.
help.is_active: Inactive controls are hidden from new product assignments.
help.requirements: CRA catalogue requirements this control helps satisfy.

products.title: Products
products.subtitle: Manage products in your organization
products.confirm_delete: Delete this product and all its versions permanently?
products.search_placeholder: Search by name, slug, type or status...

campaigns.description: Track patch rollout across customer installations

assistant.description: Ask CRA questions grounded in this product (human review required)

sdl.description: Secure development lifecycle runs and release gates

passport.title: Product Compliance Passport
passport.subtitle: Single-page CRA readiness overview for this product
passport.disclaimer: This passport summarises process status and evidence. It does not constitute automatic or legally guaranteed CRA conformity.
passport.tech_docs_intro: Status of the §5.12 technical documentation workspace for this product — published package, section completeness, and linked USI / SDL references.

repository.connection_help: Organization GitHub or GitLab connection used to access the repository.
repository.help: Use owner/repo (or group/project for GitLab) or a GitHub/GitLab URL.
repository.unlink_confirm: The product will no longer be linked to this repository. Sync history for this link will be removed.
repository.no_connection: Connect GitHub or GitLab in Settings → Integrations before linking a repository.
repository.sync_failed: Repository sync failed. Check the last sync details.
repository.invalid_format: Enter a valid repository as owner/repo (or group/project) or a GitHub/GitLab URL.
repository.not_found: Repository not found with the connected token.
repository.fetch_failed: Could not fetch repository details from the provider.
repository.connection_unavailable: The selected VCS connection is not available.

help.name: Official product name used in registers, reports and release documentation.
help.slug: URL-safe identifier unique within the organization. Leave empty to generate from the name.
help.product_line: Optional grouping for related products (e.g. a suite or family).
help.description: Short overview of what the product does for stakeholders and auditors.
help.intended_purpose: Describe what the product is designed for, who uses it, the environment it runs in, and which external systems it interacts with.
help.product_type: Whether the product is software, hardware, or another type for regulatory context.
help.manufacturer: Legal manufacturer name as placed on the market.
help.trademark: Brand or trade name under which the product is offered. A registered trademark is not required.
help.licensing_model: How the product is monetised: paid, free, or indirectly monetised.
help.remote_processing: Whether remote data processing is required for the product to function.
help.network_connectivity: Whether the product has logical or physical network connectivity.
help.deployment_model: Product-level architecture (e.g. SaaS, on-prem, plugin, appliance)—not a registry of customer installations.
help.support_period_notes: Free-text notes about how long and under what terms support is provided.
help.end_of_support_policy: Policy describing what happens when support ends (security fixes, withdrawals, etc.).
help.product_owner: Organization member accountable for the product lifecycle and roadmap.
help.security_contact: Organization member to contact for security and vulnerability reports.
help.scope_status: Current CRA scope assessment outcome for this product.
help.scope_rationale: Describe the facts and assumptions behind the current scope status. Cover product type, how it is made available, network or system connections, commercial context, and any possible exclusions. For a preliminary assessment, note that it still requires confirmation.
help.classification_status: CRA product classification category (general, important, critical, etc.).
help.classification_rationale: Explain why the product falls into the selected CRA category. Cover its main functions, whether it has a cybersecurity purpose, and whether it matches a category in Annex III or Annex IV.
help.next_review: Date when classification should be reviewed again.

scope.out_of_scope: Out of scope based on current data

versions.title: Versions
versions.subtitle: Manage product versions and release states
versions.search_placeholder: Search by version, state or git ref...

help.version_number: Enter the unique version number under which this release was published and made available to users, for example 1.9.0.
help.release_date: Date when this version was or will be released to customers.
help.state: Current lifecycle state of the version (draft, review, released, withdrawn, etc.).
help.support_status: Whether this version is fully supported, security-only, or unsupported.
help.security_support_deadline: Last date until which security fixes are planned for this version.
help.git_ref: Enter the Git tag or commit hash that uniquely identifies the source code used to create this version.
help.build_identifier: Enter a unique identifier for the specific build or installation package. It must distinguish this build from other builds of the same product version.
help.artifact_hash: Checksum/hash of the release package for integrity verification.
help.changelog: Human-readable summary of changes included in this version.
help.previous_version: Earlier version that this release supersedes, if applicable.

support_periods.title: Support periods
support_periods.subtitle: Commercial and security support windows for this product
support_periods.search_placeholder: Search by type, start basis, versions...
support_periods.no_versions: Create product versions first to link them here.
support_periods.release_schedule_hint: The calendar window is calculated from the earliest release date of the linked versions.

help.type: Select the support type. Commercial covers contractual and functional product support. Security covers the period during which vulnerabilities are handled and security updates are provided.
help.start_basis: What event starts this support window. Calendar status is calculated only for Release date when linked versions have a release date.
help.duration_months: How many months the support commitment lasts after the start basis event.
help.basis: Describe the criteria and facts used to determine the length of the support period. For Commercial, state the licensing and contractual terms. For Security, state the expected lifecycle, product purpose, user expectations, and applicable regulatory requirements.
help.exceptions_notes: Describe any agreed deviations from the standard support period for specific customers, versions, or installations. If there are none, state "No customer exceptions".
help.versions: Product versions covered by this support period.

deployments.title: Customer deployments
deployments.subtitle: Installations of this product at customer sites
deployments.search_placeholder: Search by customer, environment or version...
deployments.environment_taken: This customer already has an installation for this environment.
deployments.customer_invalid: Selected customer is not valid for this organization.

help.environment: One installation line per customer and environment (production, staging, other). Distinct from the product delivery model field.
help.file: UTF-8 CSV with comma separator. Customers must already exist unless imported separately.

deployments.import_subtitle: Bulk create or update installations for this product from CSV
deployments.columns_hint: Required: customer_name or customer_external_ref, environment. Optional: version_number, installation_date, internet_exposure, update_channel, last_confirmed_at, custom_modifications, end_of_support_exception, notes.
deployments.completed: Import finished: :created created, :updated updated, :skipped skipped, :errors row errors.
deployments.all_rows_failed: No rows could be imported. Check the CSV format and values.
deployments.missing_columns: The CSV must include environment and customer_name or customer_external_ref.
deployments.invalid_environment: Environment must be production, staging, or other.
deployments.invalid_version: Version number does not exist for this product.
deployments.customer_not_found: Customer was not found in this organization.
deployments.unsupported_subtitle: Customer installations on versions without active support
deployments.unsupported_hint: Lists installations whose confirmed version is marked unsupported or past its security support deadline. Installations with a documented end-of-support exception are excluded.

campaigns.title: Patch campaigns
campaigns.subtitle: Track customer update rollout for this product
campaigns.activated: Campaign activated and matching installations attached.
campaigns.completed: Campaign completed — all targets are updated or excepted.
campaigns.completed_empty: Campaign completed — no installations needed an update.
campaigns.notifications_queued: Queued :queued email notification(s); skipped :skipped without contact email.
campaigns.confirm_notify: Queue stub email notifications for pending targets that have a contact email? Targets without an email are skipped. Delivery uses the configured mailer (provider credentials later).
campaigns.draft_dialog_description: Creates an AI draft from this campaign context. Human review is required — nothing is sent or closed automatically.
campaigns.confirm_activate: Activate this campaign and attach all installations that are not yet on the target version?
campaigns.search_placeholder: Search by title, status or version...
campaigns.only_draft: Only draft campaigns can be changed.
campaigns.only_active_targets: Target status can only be updated on active campaigns.
campaigns.updated_sync_hint: Marking as updated will set the installation confirmed version to this campaign’s target version.
campaigns.version_invalid: Selected version is not valid for this product.
campaigns.vulnerability_invalid: Selected vulnerability is not valid for this product.
campaigns.seed_hint: On activate, installations with no version or a different version than the target are attached as pending targets.
campaigns.targets_draft_hint: Targets are attached when the campaign is activated.
campaigns.targets_empty: No matching installations were attached (all are already on the target version).

help.title: Short name for this rollout campaign.
help.target_version: Customers should move to this product version.
help.vulnerability: Optional link to the vulnerability that triggered this campaign.

campaigns.notification_log_empty: No notification events recorded for this target yet.

scope_wizard.title: CRA scope assessment
scope_wizard.subtitle: Answer 12 questions for a preliminary CRA scope suggestion. This is not a final legal determination.
scope_wizard.override_hint: You can override the suggestion before saving.
scope_wizard.no_assessment: No wizard assessment has been recorded yet.
scope_wizard.preview_error: Could not evaluate answers. Check that all questions are answered.

product_kind.label: Is this a software or hardware product?
product_kind.help: Select the primary product kind for CRA context.

commercial_activity.label: Is it made available in the course of a commercial activity?
commercial_activity.help: Commercial activity includes paid offerings and many free products with a commercial intent.

network_or_device_link.label: Does it have a logical or physical connection to a device or network?
network_or_device_link.help: Includes network interfaces, device linking, or remote connectivity.

offered_standalone.label: Is it offered as a standalone product?
offered_standalone.help: Whether customers can obtain it on its own, not only as an inseparable part of something else.

sold_under_own_brand.label: Is it sold under your organisation's name or brand?
sold_under_own_brand.help: Relevant for manufacturer / own-brand placing on the market.

remote_processing_required.label: Is remote data processing required for the product to function?
remote_processing_required.help: Remote processing without which the product cannot operate as intended.

other_sector_regulation.label: Is it subject to other sector-specific regulation?
other_sector_regulation.help: For example medical devices, aviation, automotive, or other specialised regimes.

component_of_other_product.label: Is it a component of another product?
component_of_other_product.help: Embedded, OEM, or otherwise integrated into a larger finished product.

free_open_source.label: Is it free and open-source software?
free_open_source.help: FOSS distribution may affect exclusions depending on commercial activity.

substantial_modification.label: Does it involve a substantial modification of another party's product?
substantial_modification.help: Substantial modification can change the economic operator role under CRA.

market_role.label: Who carries the role of manufacturer, importer, or distributor?
market_role.help: Select the organisation's primary economic operator role for this product.

offered_in_eu.label: Is it offered on the European Union market?
offered_in_eu.help: Placing or making available on the EU market is a core CRA trigger.

classification_wizard.title: CRA product classification
classification_wizard.subtitle: Answer Annex III/IV oriented questions for a preliminary classification. This is not a final legal determination.
classification_wizard.override_hint: You can override the suggestion before saving.
classification_wizard.no_assessment: No classification assessment has been recorded yet.
classification_wizard.preview_error: Could not evaluate answers. Check that all questions are answered.

help.regulatory_content_version: Label for the CRA annex / guidance version used for this assessment (stub until the requirements library exists).
help.evidence_notes: Optional notes or references supporting the classification (stub until the evidence repository exists).

identity_access_security.label: Does the product provide identity, access control, password management or MFA functions?
identity_access_security.help: Annex III Class I signals: identity management systems, password managers, access control.

network_security.label: Does it provide firewall, VPN, IDS/IPS or network management security functions?
network_security.help: Annex III Class I network security category products.

endpoint_security.label: Does it provide antivirus, EDR, boot management or similar endpoint security?
endpoint_security.help: Annex III Class I endpoint / boot security products.

browser_or_runtime.label: Is it a standalone or embedded browser (or comparable runtime for web content)?

operating_system.label: Is it an operating system for desktops, servers or mobile devices?
operating_system.help: Annex III Class II operating systems.

hypervisor_containers.label: Is it a hypervisor or container runtime?
hypervisor_containers.help: Annex III Class II virtualisation / container runtimes.

pki_crypto.label: Does it provide PKI, HSM, secure element or smartcard-like cryptographic trust services?
pki_crypto.help: May indicate Important Class I PKI products or Critical Annex IV secure elements — escalate carefully.

critical_infrastructure.label: Is it intended for critical / essential entities, smart metering or industrial control?
critical_infrastructure.help: Annex IV critical product signals and high-impact deployments.

sector_specific_regime.label: Is the product primarily subject to other sector-specific legislation instead?
sector_specific_regime.help: Medical, aviation, automotive and similar regimes may lead to sector-specific classification.

explicitly_excluded.label: Is the product clearly excluded from CRA product classification?
explicitly_excluded.help: Use only when exclusion is already established; otherwise prefer under review.

controls.title: Product controls
controls.subtitle: Controls applied to this product and their status
controls.confirm_remove: Remove this control from the product?
controls.search_placeholder: Search by code, name or status...
controls.no_available: All active controls are already assigned to this product.

help.control: Choose a control from the organization library.
help.status: Current implementation status of this control on the product.
help.notes: Optional notes about gaps, owners or next steps.

risks.title: Risk register
risks.subtitle: Cybersecurity risks for this product
risks.search_placeholder: Search by title, category or status...
risks.no_controls: No active controls in the organization library yet.

help.title: Short name for the risk entry.
help.category: Primary threat category for reporting and filtering.
help.status: Lifecycle status of this risk record.
help.asset: What is at risk (product function, data, interface, etc.).
help.threat: Who or what may cause harm.
help.weakness: Vulnerability, design gap or weakness enabling the threat.
help.attack_scenario: How the attack or failure could unfold.
help.impact: Estimated business or safety impact before additional treatment.
help.residual_likelihood: Likelihood after planned or applied treatment.
help.residual_impact: Impact after planned or applied treatment.
help.treatment_plan: Actions to mitigate, transfer or avoid the risk.
help.owner: Organization member accountable for this risk.
help.deadline: Target date for treatment or review.
help.product_version: Optional product version this risk applies to.
help.controls: Controls that already reduce this risk.
help.requirements: CRA requirements related to this risk.

components.title: Component inventory
components.subtitle: SBOM components for product versions
components.import_subtitle: Import CycloneDX JSON or composer.lock into a product version
components.search_placeholder: Search by name, version, ecosystem or purl...

help.product_version: Components belong to a specific product release.
help.filter_version: Limit the inventory to components of one product version.
help.name: Package or component name.
help.package_ecosystem: Package manager or origin of the component.
help.version: Exact version string from the SBOM or lockfile.
help.purl: Package URL used for upsert matching on re-import.
help.is_direct: Whether this is a direct dependency of the product.
help.is_dev: Whether this is only used in development or build tooling.
help.usage_context: How the component is used (runtime, library, plugin, etc.).
help.support_status: Known support lifecycle for this component.
help.notes: Free-form notes for compliance or review.
help.format: Leave empty to detect CycloneDX JSON or composer.lock automatically.
help.file: Upload a CycloneDX JSON or composer.lock file (max 10 MB).

vulnerabilities.title: Vulnerability register
vulnerabilities.subtitle: Track vulnerabilities and reporting deadlines for this product
vulnerabilities.search_placeholder: Search by title, CVE, status or severity...
vulnerabilities.no_components: No components in the inventory yet.
vulnerabilities.patch_campaigns_subtitle: Customer rollout campaigns linked to this vulnerability
vulnerabilities.patch_campaigns_empty: No patch campaigns are linked yet.
vulnerabilities.ai_triage_dialog_description: Creates AI suggestions for severity, versions, and remediation. Human review is required — nothing is applied or closed automatically.

help.title: Short name for the vulnerability record.
help.summary: What is wrong and why it matters.
help.advisory_url: Link to vendor or public advisory.
help.discovered_at: When the issue was first discovered.
help.awareness_at: When the organization became aware — used for 24h/72h deadlines.
help.cvss_score: Optional CVSS base score from 0.0 to 10.0.
help.exploitation_status: Whether exploitation is known in the wild.
help.is_public: Whether the issue is already public.
help.workaround: Temporary mitigation until a fix is available.
help.owner: Organization member accountable for this vulnerability.
help.substitute_owner: Backup owner for escalation when primary is unavailable.
help.corrective_measure_available_at: When a corrective measure became available — starts the 14-day final report deadline for actively exploited issues.
help.notes: Internal notes for triage and follow-up.
help.components: SBOM components affected by this vulnerability.
help.fixed_versions: Product versions that include the fix.

reporting.title: Reporting workflow
reporting.subtitle: Track 24h / 72h / final reporting deadlines and submission records
reporting.disclaimer: This workflow helps organise CRA reporting steps. It does not submit to ENISA SRP and does not guarantee legal compliance.
reporting.escalation_banner: One or more reporting milestones are overdue.
reporting.save_before_submit: Save the draft first, then submit for approval.
reporting.unsaved_changes_hint: You have unsaved changes. Save the draft before submitting for approval.
reporting.complete_required_fields: Fill in all required fields for this reporting stage before submitting.

validation.affected_versions_required: Affected versions are required for this stage.
validation.corrective_or_workaround_required: Corrective action or workaround is required for the final report.

summaries.corrective_date_missing: Set corrective measure availability to calculate the final deadline.

help.summary: Short description of the issue for this reporting milestone.
help.impact: Potential or observed impact on products, users or systems.
help.workaround: Temporary mitigation available to users, if any.
help.corrective_or_workaround: For the final report, provide a corrective action or a workaround (at least one).
help.contact_email: Contact email for follow-up on the report.
help.notes: Internal notes that are not part of the formal submission.
help.approval_comment: Optional comment recorded with approve or reject.
help.channel: How the report was sent outside the system (email, portal, other).
help.reference: External ticket, email ID or portal receipt reference.

help.title: Short name for the evidence item.

help.collected_at: When the evidence was collected or generated.
help.freshness_status: Manual override for superseded or invalid; otherwise derived from dates.
help.supersedes: Previous evidence item this one replaces.
help.file_replace: Leave empty to keep the current file, or upload a replacement.

readiness.title: Readiness report
readiness.subtitle: Gap checklist and detailed readiness sections
readiness.disclaimer: This report organizes readiness evidence and decisions. It does not constitute automatic or legally guaranteed CRA conformity.

identification.complete: Name, type and manufacturer are set.

versions.ok: At least one non-draft version exists.

support.documented: Structured support periods are in place.
support.partial: Support periods need attention (ending soon or incomplete).
support.ending_critical: Support ends within 30 days or has ended.

requirements.none: No requirements assessed for this product.
requirements.partial: Some requirements are still not assessed.
requirements.assessed: All linked requirements have been assessed.

controls.none: No controls assigned to this product.

sbom.present: SBOM or component inventory is present.

policies.missing: One or more required policy types lack an approved document.
policies.review_due: All required types are approved; policies are still under review.
policies.complete: All required policy types have an approved document.

security_instructions.missing: No published user security instructions for this product.
security_instructions.published: Published user security instructions are available.

sdl.missing: In-scope release is awaiting SDL release security approval.
sdl.approved: SDL release security approval covers versions in security review / release candidate.
sdl.no_release_in_progress: No version is in security review or release candidate.

deployments.unresolved_high: Active patch campaign still has high-criticality installations not updated.
deployments.campaigns_clear: Active patch campaigns have no unresolved high-criticality targets.
deployments.no_active_campaign: No active patch campaign for customer installations.
deployments.unsupported_versions: Customer installations remain on unsupported or expired versions.

evidence.review_due: Some evidence is due for review.

responsible_persons.both: Product owner and security contact are set.
responsible_persons.partial: At least one responsible person is set.
responsible_persons.missing: No product owner or security contact.

release.not_available: Releases module is not available yet.
release.awaiting_approval: Versions are waiting in security review or release candidate.
release.has_releases: At least one approved or released version exists.
release.none: No approved or released versions yet.

technical_documentation.published: A published technical documentation package is available.
technical_documentation.usi_unlinked: Technical documentation is published but has no linked published USI.
technical_documentation.incomplete: The latest published package has incomplete applicable sections.
technical_documentation.draft_or_review: Technical documentation exists only as draft or under review.
technical_documentation.missing: No published technical documentation package yet.
technical_documentation.not_required: Technical documentation is not required for out-of-scope products.

repository.not_linked: No GitHub repository is linked to this product.
repository.linked_synced: Repository is linked and has been synced.
repository.ci_failing: CI on the default branch is failing.

reporting.submissions_recorded: At least one reporting submission is recorded.
reporting.in_progress: Vulnerabilities with awareness timestamps await reporting steps.

gaps.generic: Review this section and complete missing data.
gaps.classification: Set a CRA classification for this product.
gaps.scope_review: Resolve further legal review for CRA scope.
gaps.versions: Add at least one product version.
gaps.versions_draft: Move a version beyond draft state.
gaps.support_partial: Review support periods ending soon or unsupported versions without deadlines.
gaps.support_ending_critical: Support ends within 30 days — update periods or plan end-of-support communication.
gaps.support_periods_missing: Add structured commercial and security support periods.
gaps.requirements: Assess CRA requirements for this product.
gaps.controls: Assign organization controls to this product.
gaps.risks: Create at least one risk assessment.
gaps.sbom: Import an SBOM or add components.
gaps.unresolved_exposed_deployments: Active patch campaign still has high-criticality customer installations that are not updated or excepted.
gaps.unsupported_deployments: Customer installations remain on unsupported versions or past security support deadlines.
gaps.policies_missing: Approve required organizational policies (CVD, SDL, support, update, incident response, third-party).
gaps.policies_review_due: Complete review of organizational policies still under review.
gaps.security_instructions_missing: Publish user security instructions for this in-scope product.
gaps.sdl_release_approval_missing: Record SDL release security approval for the in-scope version in security review or release candidate.
gaps.evidence: Add compliance evidence for this product.
gaps.responsible_persons: Assign a product owner or security contact.
gaps.release_awaiting: Advance versions out of security review / release candidate.
gaps.technical_documentation: Publish a technical documentation package for this product.
gaps.technical_documentation_missing: Publish a technical documentation package for this in-scope product.
gaps.technical_documentation_incomplete: Complete all applicable sections on the published technical documentation package.
gaps.technical_documentation_usi_unlinked: Link a published user security instruction to the technical documentation package.
gaps.no_repository_linked: Link a GitHub repository on the product edit page.
gaps.ci_failing: Fix failing CI on the linked repository default branch, then sync again.

assistant.disclaimer: The AI assistant is a helper, not an autonomous compliance authority. It must not determine final CRA applicability, confirm compliance, close vulnerabilities, submit reports, or change product data. All material decisions require human review and approval.
assistant.empty: Ask a question about this product’s compliance context. Responses are suggestions only.
assistant.input_placeholder: Ask about requirements, controls, evidence, or gaps…

analyse.title: Document analyser

analyse.note_placeholder: e.g. Focus on vulnerability disclosure coverage…
analyse.human_review: Suggestions only — human review required. Nothing is applied automatically.

draft.human_review: Draft only — human review required. Nothing is sent or closed automatically.
draft.subject: Subject

triage.human_review: Suggestions only — human review required. Nothing is applied or closed automatically.

queue.pending: Analysis queued — waiting for a worker…
queue.failed: Queued analysis failed. You can retry from the source action.

help.title_override: Optional override for the default section title.
help.use_template: Prefill document title and all section bodies with EN/BG starter content for the selected locale.

user_security_instructions.ai_draft_error: Could not generate an AI draft for this section.

user_security_instructions.pair_product_mismatch: Translations must belong to the same product.

incidents.title: Security incident register
incidents.subtitle: Track security incidents separately from vulnerabilities for this product
incidents.search_placeholder: Search by title, status or severity...

help.title: Short name for the incident record.
help.summary: What happened and why it matters.
help.confidentiality_impact: Impact on confidentiality (CVSS-style: none / low / high).
help.integrity_impact: Impact on integrity (CVSS-style: none / low / high).
help.availability_impact: Impact on availability (CVSS-style: none / low / high).
help.attack_vector: How the incident was reachable (network / adjacent / local / physical).
help.owner: Organization member accountable for this incident.
help.actual_started_at: When the security event actually started.
help.detected_at: When the event was first detected.
help.awareness_at: When the organization became aware of the incident.
help.classified_at: When the event was classified as a security incident.
help.corrective_measures: Actions taken or planned to contain and remediate.
help.lessons_evidence: Optional evidence records that document lessons learned.
help.lessons_controls: Optional controls reinforced or introduced from lessons learned.
help.notes: Internal notes for investigation and follow-up.
help.versions: Product versions impacted by this incident.
help.customers: Organization customers impacted by this incident.
help.deployments: Product deployments impacted by this incident.
help.timeline_label: Short label, e.g. Customer report or Mitigation applied.
help.timeline_notes: Optional details for this timeline entry. Events cannot be edited after save.
help.vulnerability_link: Choose an existing vulnerability on this product to link to the incident.
help.create_approval_task: Creates an open task linked to this incident for post-closure review.
help.report_authority: Who received the report (e.g. national CSIRT, ENISA SRP).
help.report_submitted_at: When the submission was sent to the authority.
help.report_reference: Ticket, receipt, or case identifier from the authority.
help.report_summary: Short description of what was submitted.
help.communication_subject: Short subject of the customer communication.
help.communication_communicated_at: When the communication was sent or held.
help.communication_audience: Free-text audience when not linked to one customer (e.g. all affected).

incidents.cia_subtitle: Optional classification aligned with CVSS impact metrics.
incidents.ai_draft_disclaimer: Draft only; human review required before save. Nothing is saved until you apply and click Save.
incidents.ai_draft_error: Could not generate an AI summary draft.
incidents.timeline_subtitle: Core timestamps and append-only investigation events.
incidents.reports_subtitle: Manual record of submissions to authorities. No auto-submit.
incidents.communications_subtitle: Manual log of incident-related customer outreach. Separate from patch campaigns.
incidents.core_timestamps_subtitle: Start, detection, awareness, and classification times for this incident.
incidents.investigation_subtitle: Root cause, corrective measures, and lessons learned for this incident.
incidents.vulnerability_subtitle: Connect this incident to a vulnerability record, or create one from the investigation.
incidents.vulnerability_create_help: Creates a vulnerability with discovery source “Incident investigation”, copies key fields, and opens it for review.
incidents.no_vulnerabilities: No vulnerabilities registered for this product yet.
incidents.confirm_close: Marks the incident as closed and records who closed it. This can be reopened later by changing the status.
incidents.closure_subtitle: Close the incident with an audit trail. Optionally create a follow-up approval task.
incidents.only_active_closable: Only open, investigating, or contained incidents can be closed.
incidents.close_requires_awareness: Set the awareness timestamp before closing the incident.
incidents.close_requires_root_cause: Record the root cause before closing the incident.
incidents.close_requires_corrective_measures: Record corrective measures before closing the incident.
incidents.close_assignee_invalid: The assignee must be a member of this organization.

Post-closure review for security incident.
Title: :title

sdl.invalid_version: The selected version does not belong to this product.

sdl.invalid_git_url: Enter a valid http(s) URL for the PR, MR, or CI run.

export.title: SDL run summary
export.disclaimer: This document is an internal secure development lifecycle summary for release package review. It is not a legal compliance certificate.
export.invalid_format: Export format must be markdown or pdf.

sdl.confirm_template_overwrite: Existing notes for this stage will be replaced by the checklist template.
sdl.approved_post_release_help: Publication and post-release monitoring stages can still be completed while this run stays approved.
sdl.approval_help: Approve only after stages through Release approval are complete and the release approval stage is marked Done. Approved status cannot be set from the status field.
sdl.confirm_revoke: The run will return to in progress and can be edited again.
sdl.approve_via_gate: Use the release security approval action instead of setting status to approved.
sdl.approved_locked: This SDL run is approved and locked. Revoke approval to edit.
sdl.approve_cancelled: A cancelled SDL run cannot be approved.
sdl.already_approved: This SDL run is already approved.
sdl.only_approved_revocable: Only approved SDL runs can have approval revoked.
sdl.approve_requires_stages: Complete all stages through Release approval (Done, N/A, or Exception) before approving.
sdl.approve_requires_release_stage_done: Mark the Release approval stage as Done before approving.
sdl.approve_not_ready: Release approval gate is not ready yet.
sdl.exception_required: Exception owner and review expiry are required when status is Exception.
sdl.exception_notes_required: Document the exception rationale in stage notes.
sdl.exception_owner_invalid: Exception owner must be an organization member.
sdl.exception_task_description:
Follow up on SDL exception for ":run" (:stage).

Rationale:
:notes
sdl.documentation_help: Optionally link a published user security instructions document and note that the technical documentation outline / delta was reviewed for this release.
sdl.usi_link_invalid: Select a published user security instructions document for this product.
sdl.usi_link_none: No published user security instructions for this product yet.

help.title: Short label for this SDL run (feature, release, or security gate).
help.status: Overall run status. Approval gate is enforced separately later.
help.current_stage: Where the run is currently focused in the §5.14 stage workflow.
help.product_version: Optional pin to a product version for this run.
help.owner: Person responsible for driving this SDL run.
help.notes: Free-form context for reviewers and auditors.
help.evidence: Product evidence supporting this SDL run overall (PR, CI, scan, test report).
help.use_template: Prefill selected stage notes with secure coding and threat checklist Markdown (optional).
help.locale: Language for checklist templates applied on create.
help.git_url: HTTPS link to a pull request, merge request, or CI run.
help.git_title: Optional label; defaults to a short path from the URL.
help.git_stage: Optionally attach the new evidence to a stage checklist as well.
help.stage_status: Pending until complete. Use N/A when the stage does not apply; exception documents a justified deviation.
help.stage_notes: Threat considerations, checklist outcomes, or rationale for N/A / exception.
help.stage_evidence: Evidence specific to this stage (peer review, scan result, test report).
help.exception_owner: Organization member accountable for resolving or reviewing this exception.
help.exception_expires_at: Date by which the exception must be reviewed or closed.
help.linked_usi: Published user security instructions that accompany this SDL / release (optional).
help.tech_doc_delta_reviewed: Confirm the technical documentation outline / version delta was reviewed (passport / readiness). Full tech-doc workspace is out of scope.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.heading: Integrations
integrations.description: Connect GitHub or GitLab so products can sync repository evidence.
integrations.connect_github_description: Connect with a personal access token or a GitHub App installation. Credentials are stored encrypted and never shown again.
integrations.connect_gitlab_description: Use a gitlab.com personal access token with read_api / read_repository. The token is stored encrypted and never shown again.
integrations.label: Label
integrations.token_help: Required scopes: repo (or public_repo) for private/public repository metadata.
integrations.github_app_id_help: Numeric App ID from the GitHub App settings page.
integrations.github_installation_id_help: Installation ID from the App installation URL or API.
integrations.github_private_key_help: Paste the .pem private key generated for the GitHub App. Stored encrypted.
integrations.github_private_key_optional_help: Leave blank to keep the existing private key.
integrations.github_app_help: Create a GitHub App with read-only repository contents, metadata, and security events; install it on the org or account that owns the repos.
integrations.gitlab_token_help: Required scopes: read_api and read_repository (or a broader api scope).
integrations.disconnect_confirm: Linked product repositories for this connection will be removed.
integrations.github_token_invalid: GitHub rejected this token. Check the token and try again.
integrations.github_app_invalid: GitHub rejected these App credentials. Check App ID, Installation ID, and private key.
integrations.github_app_private_key_invalid: The private key is not a valid PEM for signing a GitHub App JWT.
integrations.github_app_private_key_required: A private key is required when connecting a GitHub App for the first time.
integrations.gitlab_token_invalid: GitLab rejected this token. Check the token and try again.
integrations.no_access: You do not have permission to manage integrations.
integrations.provider_not_connected: This provider is not connected yet.
integrations.sync_schedule_help: Applies to all product repositories linked through this connection. The scheduler runs hourly and syncs only when due.
integrations.webhook_help: Create a repository or organization webhook in GitHub pointing to this URL. Events: release, create (tags), workflow_run, dependabot_alert, push. Content type: application/json.
integrations.webhook_not_configured: Not configured — generate a secret first
integrations.webhook_secret_rotated: Webhook secret generated. Copy it now — it will not be shown again.
integrations.webhook_secret_once_help: Paste this into GitHub as the webhook secret. It is shown only once.

appearance.label: Appearance
appearance.settings_description: Update the appearance settings for your account

assistant.disabled: The AI assistant is disabled. Set CRA_AI_ENABLED=true to use it.
assistant.stub_provider_note: Local stub provider — not a live model. Human review required.
assistant.message_required: Enter a message to send to the assistant.
assistant.conversation_forbidden: You can only continue your own AI conversations.
assistant.provider_misconfigured: The AI provider is not configured. Set the API key for the selected CRA_AI_PROVIDER.
assistant.provider_failed: The AI provider request failed. Try again or switch back to the stub provider.

analyse.unsupported_type: Unsupported file type. Upload a text document (txt, md, csv, json, xml, html, log).
analyse.empty_file: The uploaded file is empty or could not be read as text.
